results.title: Building a self-driving laboratory for controlled synthesis of inorganic nanomaterials
results.summary: A self-driving laboratory is an autonomous experiment platform that conducts machine learning selected experiments to achieve a user-defined objective. Recent advances have enabled self-driving laboratories to successfully optimise the synthesis parameters such as solvent, precursor, and temperature based on measurements of the material’s properties. However, the underlying atomic structure of the material remains largely unknown. To address this, I propose integrating structure characterisation techniques such as scattering and spectroscopy into the self-driving laboratories. ML will solve the atomic structure based on the data from these experiments combined with ML-driven quantum mechanical (QM) potentials (Figure 2). The solved atomic structure will be automatically fed into an active learning framework that will suggest the next experiment enabling a directed synthesis of functional materials via insight at the atomic level.

results.title: Efficient Discovery of Novel Photoredox Reactions: A Multipurpose Self-Driving Laboratory Approach
